headline: Ethereum V3 core Pool is explorer-verified, repo-published, and covered by recent recognized v3.6 audits

findings.text: The Ethereum Aave V3 Core Pool proxy at 0x87870Bca3F3fD6335C3F4ce8392D69350B4fA4E2 is marked Source Code Verified Exact Match on Etherscan, and the page identifies the current implementation as 0x8147b99D4900f52B110B0808df199aAca60F119Bd.

Aave documentation states that the Aave v3 Protocol contracts are available on GitHub, and the aave-dao/aave-v3-origin repository contains the Pool contract source corresponding to the explorer-visible Aave V3 Pool structure; I did not perform an independent bytecode diff.

Aave's official security page lists V3.6 audits dated November 16, November 18, and November 29, 2025; the v3.6 Part 2 governance proposal states the upgrade updates Pool, PoolConfigurator, AToken, and VariableDebtToken implementations on Mainnet Core and other pools.
The v3.6 audit set includes Certora and MixBytes, both recognized Solidity security firms, alongside Blackthorn, Savant, and Pashov.

The active Ethereum Core Pool proxy shows an upgrade on January 16, 2026, while Aave's V3.6 audit set is dated November 2025 and the v3.6 Part 2 proposal explicitly covers Mainnet Core Pool implementation upgrades, so observed drift from the latest audit set is under six months.

The assessed Pool is an upgradeable proxy; Etherscan identifies the active implementation address and displays implementation-contract source files in the same verified contract view, but I did not separately retrieve the standalone implementation-address page.

steelman.red: The strongest red argument is that the Pool is upgradeable and the standalone implementation-address explorer page was not separately retrieved, so a strict reviewer could demand direct implementation-page evidence rather than relying on the proxy's verified view.
steelman.orange: The strongest orange argument is that Aave has frequent production upgrades and the current v3.6 implementation was deployed after the audit dates, so the source-to-deployed-code correspondence should ideally be pinned by an independent bytecode diff.
steelman.green: The strongest green argument is that the main Ethereum Pool proxy is explorer-verified, the implementation is identified through the proxy view, the public Aave v3 source repository corresponds to the visible Pool architecture, and the current v3.6 upgrade is backed by recent Certora and MixBytes audits covering the upgraded implementation set.

rationale.verdict: Choosing green because the assessed Ethereum V3 Core Pool proxy is verified on Etherscan, its active implementation is exposed through the verified proxy view, Aave publishes the corresponding v3 source repository and deployed-address documentation, and the current v3.6 upgrade path is covered by recent recognized audits from Certora and MixBytes within the six-month drift window.

evidence.shows: Ethereum Aave V3 Core Pool proxy; Etherscan marks Source Code Verified Exact Match, identifies the contract as InitializableImmutableAdminUpgradeabilityProxy, and shows implementation 0x8147b99D4900f52B110B0808df199aAca60F119Bd with recent proxy upgrade history.
Ethereum Aave V3 PoolAddressesProvider; Etherscan marks Source Code Verified Exact Match and identifies contract name PoolAddressesProvider.

Aave v3 smart-contract documentation states that v3 source code is available on GitHub, describes Pool as the main user entry point, states Pool is owned by the PoolAddressesProvider, and states PoolAddressesProvider can update proxy implementations.
Aave Pool documentation states that most user interactions occur through Pool and that Pool supports supply, withdraw, borrow, repay, collateral configuration, liquidation, and flash loans.
Public source repository contains Aave v3 Pool.sol with supply, withdraw, borrow, repay, flash loan, liquidation, and v3.6-era functions such as eliminateReserveDeficit.
Aave's official security page lists official audit reports and verifications; V3.6 audits include reports dated Nov 16, Nov 18, and Nov 29, 2025.

Aave governance proposal 433 states that V3.6 Part 2 updates Pool, PoolConfigurator, AToken, and VariableDebtToken implementations on Mainnet Core and other pools and lists Blackthorn, Certora, MixBytes, Savant, and Pashov audits.
Aave v3.6 Certora audit report file in the public aave-v3-origin repository at commit 9f69c714350635787439608b52fb6064ce991bba.
Aave v3.6 MixBytes audit report file in the public aave-v3-origin repository at commit 9f69c714350635787439608b52fb6064ce991bba.
Aave v3.6 Blackthorn audit report file in the public aave-v3-origin repository at commit 9f69c714350635787439608b52fb6064ce991bba.
Aave v3.6 Savant audit report file in the public aave-v3-origin repository at commit 9f69c714350635787439608b52fb6064ce991bba.
Aave v3.6 Pashov audit report file in the public aave-v3-origin repository at commit 9f69c714350635787439608b52fb6064ce991bba.
Aave documentation provides a deployed-contracts address dashboard and points integrators to the Aave Address Book package.
Primary Aave governance forum for public governance discussion.
Ethereum AAVE ERC-20 token contract page showing token contract address and 18 decimals.
Aave Immunefi bug bounty page describes Aave as a decentralized non-custodial liquidity protocol and lists maximum bounty, live-since date, and last-updated date.

unknowns: V2: I did not perform an independent local compile or bytecode diff between the deployed Pool implementation and the GitHub source.
V3: The prompt's pinned audit_links field was empty, so I used Aave's official security page and governance proposal instead of a pinned audit URL.

V6: I did not separately retrieve the standalone Etherscan page for implementation 0x8147b99D4900f52B110B0808df199aAca60F119Bd; I relied on the proxy page's implementation disclosure and verified source view.

admin_addresses.role: Ethereum V3 PoolAddressesProvider; owner/admin registry for the Pool proxy and market components

protocol_metadata.about: Aave is a decentralized non-custodial liquidity protocol where users supply assets to earn interest or borrow against collateral from shared liquidity markets. The V3 Pool is the main user-facing contract for supply, withdrawal, borrowing, repayment, liquidation, and flash loans, while risk parameters and market components are managed through Aave's address-provider and configurator architecture. AAVE is the Ethereum governance token used in the protocol's governance system.
